message:
fix(mux/tmux): preserve in-flight head during cmd_queue cap drain (ft-wajba)

push_command_capped used queue.drain(..excess) unconditionally to
enforce CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH. Under State::WaitingForResponse, index 0
of the cmd_queue is the in-flight command: send_next_command wrote it
to the pane but did NOT pop it — the pop happens in the Guarded response
handler, which calls cmd_queue.pop_front() and feeds the response to
cmd.process_result.

If push_command_capped ran while state == WaitingForResponse and the
queue grew past CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH = 50_000 (pathological: remote tmux
lag + LayoutChange/SessionChanged event storm), the drain silently
dropped the in-flight command. When the Guarded response then arrived,
pop_front() popped a DIFFERENT pending command and handed the response
payload to a parser that never issued the request — cross-wiring
response routing in a way that corrupts every subsequent command parser
bumped into the head slot. The bug surfaces as silent command failures,
not crashes.

Root cause is that the original ft-4qom2 push-site cap enforcement
(1055cffc) traded unbounded memory for this correctness edge without
consulting state.

Fix:

- Convert push_command_capped from a free function to a method on
  TmuxDomainState so it can consult self.state. All four call sites
  inside impl TmuxDomainState were already &self-available.
- Under state == WaitingForResponse: drain `1..=excess` (preserves
  index 0, the in-flight head).
- Under any other state (Idle, WaitForInitialGuard, Exit): retain the
  existing `..excess` drop-oldest semantics — there is no in-flight
  head at index 0 to protect.
- Log message distinguishes the two drain paths so a future operator
  debugging an event-storm incident can tell which discipline engaged.
- Lock ordering: method acquires state transiently while the caller
  holds cmd_queue. Matches the Guarded-handler lock order
  (cmd_queue first, state second) — no deadlock, no new critical
  section.

send_next_command's drain at the pop site is unchanged: it is guarded
by `if *self.state.lock() != State::Idle { return; }` at the function
head, so by construction no in-flight command sits at index 0 when that
drain runs.

Regression coverage (mux::tmux::tests):

- cmd_queue_cap_preserves_in_flight_head_under_waiting_for_response:
  seats a distinguishable SplitPane command at index 0, flips state
  to WaitingForResponse, pushes CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH additional
  ListCommands through the method under test, then asserts (a) cap is
  respected and (b) index 0's get_command() text is still the
  SplitPane sentinel. Pre-fix this fails — index 0 is a ListCommands
  after the drain.
- cmd_queue_cap_drops_oldest_when_not_waiting_for_response: companion
  test pinning that state == Idle still uses drop-oldest (the
  distinguishable head is expected to be drained out). Protects
  against a future refactor that over-corrects and preserves the
  head under all states.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue -- --nocapture:
    cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded ... ok
    cmd_queue_cap_preserves_in_flight_head_under_waiting_for_response ... ok
    cmd_queue_cap_drops_oldest_when_not_waiting_for_response ... ok
  3 passed; 0 failed.

Unrelated pre-existing failure in proptest_pane_serde (17-tuple too
large for proptest::Strategy impl) blocks cargo check --tests for the
whole mux crate; does not affect the patched code path. Not addressed
here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
